Document [12] (Title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1494) A gestational age of 29‑30 weeks completed is associated with a liveborn infant, indicating that pregnancies reaching this stage can result in a live birth.
Document [13] (Title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2304) Early, first or unspecified trimester hemorrhage is classified as spotting complicating pregnancy, unspecified trimester.
Document [14] (Title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 4 -- Community 2848) حَمْلٌ بُوقِيّ and ربط البوق are medical concepts that frequently co‑occur, indicating that the presence of حَمْلٌ بُوقِيّ is often associated with the finding or procedure ربط البوق. This consistent association suggests that clinicians should be aware of the link between these two entities, as recognizing one may prompt evaluation for the other, thereby informing diagnosis and guiding appropriate patient care.
Document [15] (Title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 4 -- Community 2849) Cesarean section is identified as a specific type of the Arabic term “الولادة القيصرية” (cesarean birth). The condition described as INFANT PRETERM LOW BIRTH WEIGHT is clinically associated with “الولادة القيصرية”, indicating that cesarean deliveries are linked to the occurrence of preterm infants with low birth weight. This connection underscores the need for careful obstetric decision‑making and targeted neonatal care when a cesarean birth is performed.
Document [16] (Title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 4 -- Community 2849) Fat emulsion is a type of dietary supplement.
Document [17] (Title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 1 -- Community 216) that connects lipid metabolism disorders, hyperlipidemia, genetic inheritance patterns, and associated metabolic effects such as early diabetes. This integrated view supports comprehensive patient assessment and coordinated care across rehabilitation and metabolic disease management.
Document [18] (Title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 1 -- Community 237) positivity intersect with these gastrointestinal pathologies. Recognizing these interconnections supports comprehensive assessment, diagnosis, and management of patients presenting with overlapping gastrointestinal and hematologic findings.
Document [19] (Title: UMLS -- Run 21 -- Level 1 -- Community 261) disease classifications, metabolic disorder taxonomy, symptom‑treatment relationships, and genetic inheritance patterns, illustrating how elevated laboratory values, metabolic disturbances, febrile presentations, and overlapping infectious, cardiovascular, endocrine, and gynecologic conditions inform comprehensive patient assessment and care.
Document [20] (Title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 4 -- Community 2839) بتر فوق الركبة (above‑knee amputation) is clinically associated with مرض السكري نوع 1 (type 1 diabetes). Patients with type 1 diabetes have a heightened risk of severe peripheral vascular disease and foot complications, which can progress to the need for an above‑knee amputation. Recognizing this association is essential for clinicians to implement early screening, aggressive glycemic control, and multidisciplinary foot‑care strategies aimed at preventing major limb loss and improving overall patient outcomes.
Document [21] (Title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 4 -- Community 2737) Dextran 75, a high‑molecular‑weight plasma volume expander, is contraindicated in patients with الاعتلال الخثري (thrombotic disease); using dextran 75 in this condition may promote clot formation and worsen clinical outcomes, so clinicians should avoid prescribing it to individuals who have thrombotic disease.
Document [22] (Title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 3 -- Community 2106) فالج is a general medical condition, and فالج طفلي represents a more specific, narrower form of فالج, indicating the infantile variant of this condition.

Document [26] (Title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 4 -- Community 2680) Short gestation, low birth weight, and fetal growth retardation are recognized as a related group of adverse perinatal outcomes and are classified together as a distinct clinical category.

Document [28] (Title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 2 -- Community 752) essential for clinicians to anticipate potential delivery complications, decide when normal delivery procedures are feasible, and implement appropriate interventions to optimize maternal and neonatal outcomes.
Document [29] (Title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 4 -- Community 2762) Sickle cell anemia is classified as داء الكريات المنجلية, and داء الكريات المنجلية is clinically associated with قساح.
Document [30] (Title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 3 -- Community 2636) The qualifier “vrozené,” which denotes a congenital origin, can be applied to several disorders, indicating that they may be present from birth. It is an allowed qualifier for ناسور مَثانِيٌّ مَهْبِلِيّ, قَرْحَة مَعِدِيّة, انسداد صفراوي خارج الكبد, فرط البروتينات الشحمية, and الْتِهابُ المثانة. Recognizing these conditions as congenital guides clinicians toward appropriate genetic evaluation, early diagnostic testing, and tailored management plans that address their lifelong nature.
Document [31] (Title: UMLS -- Run 30 -- Level 1 -- Community 157) Overall, the model demonstrates that gestational age, birth weight, and fetal growth metrics intersect with diagnostic classifications, perinatal disease categories, and health‑service utilization, providing a comprehensive framework for understanding, classifying, and managing conditions that arise around the time of birth.
